Defines memory layout constants and stubs for MPU functions in the Pebble firmware tests.

# Purpose
This code is a C++ header file that defines constants and functions related to memory management and protection. It includes declarations for several `uint32_t` constants that represent memory boundaries and sizes, such as `__app_stack_start__`, `__app_stack_end__`, and `__FLASH_size__`. These constants are likely used to define the layout of memory regions in an embedded system. The file also declares three functions: [`mpu_enable`](<#mpu_enable>), [`mpu_get_region`](<#mpu_get_region>), and [`mpu_set_region`](<#mpu_set_region>). The [`mpu_enable`](<#mpu_enable>) function is intended to enable the Memory Protection Unit (MPU), although it currently has no implementation. The [`mpu_get_region`](<#mpu_get_region>) function returns an `MpuRegion` structure for a specified region number, and [`mpu_set_region`](<#mpu_set_region>) is intended to configure a memory region with the given `MpuRegion` structure. The file includes the `kernel/memory_layout.h` header, suggesting it is part of a larger system related to kernel memory management.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `kernel/memory_layout.h`


# Global Variables

---
### \_\_app\_stack\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t``
- **Description**: Defines the starting address of the application stack in memory. It is a constant unsigned 32-bit integer.
- **Use**: Used to mark the beginning of the application stack memory region.


---
### \_\_app\_stack\_end\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t``
- **Description**: Defines the end address of the application stack in memory. It is a constant unsigned 32-bit integer.
- **Use**: Used to mark the boundary of the application stack in memory layout.


---
### \_\_isr\_stack\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t``
- **Description**: Defines the start address of the stack used for interrupt service routines (ISRs).
- **Use**: Used to specify the memory location where the ISR stack begins.


---
### \_\_kernel\_bg\_stack\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t``
- **Description**: Represents the starting address of the kernel background stack in memory. It is a constant unsigned 32-bit integer.
- **Use**: Used to define the memory layout for the kernel's background stack.


---
### \_\_kernel\_main\_stack\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``uint32_t``
- **Description**: Defines the start address of the main stack used by the kernel. This variable is a constant and is used to specify the memory location where the kernel's main stack begins.
- **Use**: Used to determine the starting point of the kernel's main stack in memory.


---
### \_\_stack\_guard\_size\_\_
- **Type**: ``uint32_t``
- **Description**: Defines the size of the stack guard in bytes. The stack guard is a protective region in memory that helps prevent stack overflow attacks by providing a buffer zone.
- **Use**: Used to specify the size of the stack guard for memory protection purposes.


---
### \_\_worker\_stack\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t``
- **Description**: Defines the start address of the worker stack in memory. It is a constant unsigned 32-bit integer.
- **Use**: Used to specify the starting point of the worker stack in the memory layout.


---
### \_\_privileged\_functions\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t``
- **Description**: Represents the starting address of the memory region allocated for privileged functions in the system.
- **Use**: Used to identify the beginning of the memory area reserved for privileged functions.


---
### \_\_privileged\_functions\_size\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t``
- **Description**: Represents the size of the memory region allocated for privileged functions in the system.
- **Use**: Used to define the size of the memory area reserved for privileged functions.


---
### \_\_unpriv\_ro\_bss\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t``
- **Description**: A constant unsigned 32-bit integer that marks the start address of the unprivileged read-only BSS (Block Started by Symbol) section in memory.
- **Use**: Used to define the starting point of the unprivileged read-only BSS section in the memory layout.


---
### \_\_unpriv\_ro\_bss\_size\_\_
- **Type**: ``uint32_t``
- **Description**: Represents the size of the read-only BSS (Block Started by Symbol) section for unprivileged code. This section typically contains uninitialized data that is set to zero at runtime.
- **Use**: Used to define the memory size allocated for the unprivileged read-only BSS section.


---
### \_\_unpriv\_ro\_data\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t``
- **Description**: Represents the starting address of the unprivileged read-only data section in memory. This variable is a constant of type `uint32_t`, indicating it holds a 32-bit unsigned integer value.
- **Use**: Used to define the beginning of the unprivileged read-only data section in the memory layout.


---
### \_\_unpriv\_ro\_data\_size\_\_
- **Type**: ``uint32_t``
- **Description**: Represents the size of the unprivileged read-only data section in memory.
- **Use**: Used to define the memory size allocated for unprivileged read-only data.


---
### \_\_APP\_RAM\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t``
- **Description**: Defines the starting address of the application RAM section in memory.
- **Use**: Used to identify the beginning of the application RAM area for memory management purposes.


---
### \_\_APP\_RAM\_size\_\_
- **Type**: ``uint32_t``
- **Description**: Represents the size of the application RAM in bytes.
- **Use**: Used to define the memory allocation for the application RAM.


---
### \_\_WORKER\_RAM\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``const uint32_t``
- **Description**: Defines the starting address of the worker RAM section in memory.
- **Use**: Used to identify the beginning of the worker RAM section for memory management purposes.


---
### \_\_WORKER\_RAM\_size\_\_
- **Type**: ``uint32_t``
- **Description**: Defines the size of the RAM allocated for worker processes in the system. It is a constant variable of type `uint32_t`, indicating it holds a 32-bit unsigned integer value.
- **Use**: Used to specify the memory size allocated for worker processes in the system.


---
### \_\_FLASH\_start\_\_
- **Type**: ``uint32_t``
- **Description**: Represents the starting address of the flash memory region in the system's memory layout. It is a constant variable, indicating that its value is set at compile time and does not change during execution.
- **Use**: Used to define the base address of the flash memory for memory management and access purposes.


---
### \_\_FLASH\_size\_\_
- **Type**: ``uint32_t``
- **Description**: Represents the size of the flash memory in the system. It is a constant variable of type `uint32_t`, indicating that its value is a 32-bit unsigned integer.
- **Use**: Used to define the size of the flash memory for memory management and configuration purposes.
